Class MethodKit

java.lang.Object
recoder.kit.MethodKit
public class MethodKit extends Object
This class implements auxiliary method related operations.

  • Method Summary

  • Method Details

    • createArguments

      public static ASTList<Expression> createArguments(ParameterContainer p)
      Creates a list of argument expressions from a parameter container. This method is usefull for creating wrapper methods since the actual parameters are taken from the given parameter list.

    • createMethodReference

      public static MethodReference createMethodReference(MethodDeclaration decl)
      Makes a method reference to the method declaration with the same actual argument names as in the declaration. For constructing adapters. Don't use a reference prefix.

      The parent role of the result is valid.

    • createMethodReference

      public static MethodReference createMethodReference(ReferencePrefix prefix, MethodDeclaration decl)
      makes a method reference to the method declaration with the same actual argument names as in the declaration. For constructing adapters. Use a reference prefix.

      The parent role of the result is valid.

    • createNew

      public static New createNew(ConstructorDeclaration decl)
      Make a new allocation corresponding to the constructor declaration with the same actual argument names as in the declaration.

    • createAbstractMethodDeclaration

      @Deprecated public static MethodDeclaration createAbstractMethodDeclaration(MethodDeclaration decl, boolean forInterface)
      Deprecated.
      not tested
      Make an new abstract method declaration from a concrete one. The given method may not be static. If the method is for an interface, any existing redundant abstract modifier is removed, otherwise its existance is ensured; any visibility modifier is removed - this changes the visibility to public.
      Parameters:
      decl - the MethodDeclaration to be made abstract
      forInterface - boolean indicating if the abstract method is for an interface
      Returns:
      the abstract method declaration

    • createAdapterMethod

      public static MethodDeclaration createAdapterMethod(ReferencePrefix delegationObject, MethodDeclaration method)
      Create a simple adapter method for a method declaration. If the method is

      m(int i, int i2) { ..}

      the created method is

      m(int i, int i2) { delegatingObject.m(i,i2); }

    • createPackerClass

      @Deprecated public static ClassDeclaration createPackerClass(String packerClassName, List<ParameterDeclaration> parameters)
      Deprecated.
      needs severe rework (AL)
      Creates a packer class for parameter list of a method. This Packer class contains one constructor which tuples all parameters of the method into an object. Works on AST elements.
      Parameters:
      packerClassName - the String with the name of the packer class
      parameters - the list of ParameterDeclarations for the packer class to wrap
      Returns:
      the class declaration of the packed class

    • getGetters

      public static List<MethodDeclaration> getGetters(SourceInfo si, FieldSpecification f)
      Query that tries to identify getter methods for the given field within the class declaration of the given field. The criteria used are quite conservative and detect obvious cases of "getters" only. A method is regarded a getter if it is defined in the class of the field, has a return type wider than the type of the field (or matching if they are primitive) and has a return statement as last top level statement of the method body referring to the field.
      Parameters:
      si - the source info service to be used.
      f - the field to find a getter for.
      Returns:
      the list of getters; may be empty if there are no getters that match the criteria in the class.

    • rename

      @Deprecated public static boolean rename(ChangeHistory ch, CrossReferenceSourceInfo xr, MethodDeclaration method, String newName)
      Deprecated.
      replaced by recoder.kit.transformation.RenameMethod
      Transformation that renames a method and all known references to it. The new name should not hide another method.
      Parameters:
      ch - the change history (may be null).
      xr - the cross referencer service.
      method - the method declaration to be renamed; may neither be null nor a constructor declaration.
      newName - the new name for the method; may not be null and must denote a valid identifier name.
      Returns:
      true, if a rename has been necessary, false otherwise.

    • getRedefinedMethods

      public static List<Method> getRedefinedMethods(Method m)
      Query that returns a list of methods that the given method directly overwrites or implements. A method that is multiply inherited (from interfaces) occurs multiple times, accordingly.
      Parameters:
      m - a method.
      Returns:
      a list of methods that are overwritten or implemented by m .

    • getRedefiningMethods

      public static List<Method> getRedefiningMethods(CrossReferenceSourceInfo xr, Method m)
      Query that returns a list of methods that redefine or implement the given method.
      Parameters:
      xr - the cross referencer service to use.
      m - a method.
      Returns:
      a list of methods that redefine or implement m.

    • isMain

      public static boolean isMain(NameInfo ni, Method m)
      Updating query that checks if the given method is a main method.
      Parameters:
      ni - the NameInfo service to use.
      m - the method to check.
      Returns:
      true if the given method has the form "public static void main(String[] ...)", false otherwise.

    • isSerializationMethod

      public static boolean isSerializationMethod(NameInfo ni, Method m)
      Updating query that checks if the given method is one of the serialization methods writeObject,readObject ,writeReplace,readResolve.
      Parameters:
      ni - the NameInfo service to use.
      m - the method to check.
      Returns:
      true if the given method is one of the serialization methods, false otherwise.

    • cloneHeader

      public static MethodDeclaration cloneHeader(MethodDeclaration md)
      Returns a deep clone of the header of the given declaration; the body of the result is null.
      Parameters:
      md - the method declaration to clone the header from.
      Returns:
      a new method declaration sharing the header with the given one.
      See Also:
      • SourceElement.deepClone()

    • getDefinedMethod

      public static Method getDefinedMethod(ClassType type, String name, List<Type> signature)
      Query returning a method locally defined in the given type with the given name and signature.
      Parameters:
      type - the class type the method might be defined in.
      name - the name of the method.
      signature - the signature of the method.
      Returns:
      the method as defined in the class type, or null if there is no match.

    • getRedefinedMethods

      public static List<Method> getRedefinedMethods(NameInfo ni, ClassType base, String name, List<Type> signature)
      Query returning the methods which a method in the given class with the given name and signature would redefine. If there are several candidates from independent interfaces, the bottom-most ones are reported. If there is a version defined in a super class, it will be the first entry in the list (position 0).
      Parameters:
      ni - the name info service to use.
      base - the class type which would contain the redefining method.
      name - the name of the possibly redefining method.
      signature - the signature of the possibly redefining method.
      Returns:
      a list of methods that are directly redefined by a method with the given name and signature; the first entry is the method inherited from a class, if any.

    • checkMethodRedefinition

      public static Problem checkMethodRedefinition(ProgramModelInfo pmi, Method redefined, Method redefining)
      Query that finds out problems if one method redefines another one. The redefining method does not have to actually redefine the other method, the query just assumes it does. The query also assumes that names and signatures of the methods will match and that both have the same class. The query will not check any contents of the redefining method, e.g. to see if private members of the super class are accessed.
      Parameters:
      pmi - a program model info to use.
      redefined - the method to be redefined.
      redefining - the method that is / would be redefining.
      Returns:
      a problem report, one of the following:
      • FinalOverwrite, if the redefined method is final;
      • DifferentReturnTypeOverwrite, if the redefining method has a different return type;
      • MorePrivateOverwrite, if the redefining method is more private;
      • NonStaticOverwrite, if the redefined method is static but the redefining is not (if both are static, no problem is reported, even though no real redefinition is taking place);
      • UncoveredExceptionsOverwrite, if the redefined method is less exceptional;
      • null, otherwise.

    • checkMethodDeclaration

      public static Problem checkMethodDeclaration(NameInfo ni, SourceInfo si, TypeDeclaration context, MethodDeclaration candidate)
      Query that finds out problems before inserting a new method declaration.
      Parameters:
      ni - the name info to use.
      si - the source info to use.
      context - the future context of the method.
      candidate - the method declaration that might be inserted.
      Returns:
      a problem report, one of the following:
      • IllegalInterfaceMember, if the context is an interface and the candidate is not a valid member;
      • IllegalName, if the name is a keyword;
      • NameConflict, if the candidate is a constructor and its name does not match the type name;
      • NameConflict, if there is a method in the context with the same name and signature;
      • FinalOverwrite, if there is a redefined method that is final;
      • DifferentReturnTypeOverwrite, if there is a redefined method with different return type;
      • MorePrivateOverwrite, if there is a redefined method that is more public;
      • NonStaticOverwrite, if there is a redefined method that is static;
      • UncoveredExceptionsOverwrite, if there is a redefined method that is less exceptional;
      • null, otherwise.

    • getReferences

      public static List<MemberReference> getReferences(CrossReferenceSourceInfo xr, Method m, NonTerminalProgramElement root, boolean scanTree)
      Query that retrieves all references to a given method that are contained within the given tree. The specified flag defines the strategy to use: either the cross reference information is filtered, or the cross reference information is collected from the tree. The filtering mode is faster if the tree contains more nodes than there are global references to the given method.
      Parameters:
      xr - the cross referencer to use.
      m - a method.
      root - the root of an arbitrary syntax tree.
      scanTree - flag indicating the search strategy; if true, local cross reference information is build, otherwise the global cross reference information is filtered.
      Returns:
      the list of references to the given method in the given tree, can be empty but not null.
      Since:
      0.63

    • getAllRelatedMethods

      public static List<Method> getAllRelatedMethods(CrossReferenceSourceInfo xrsi, ClassType type, String methodName, List<Type> signature)
      Query that returns a list of methods would redefine, implement or are overriden or implemented each other starting from method methodName in type with specified signature . The method does not have to actually exist in type the query just assumes it does.
      Parameters:
      xrsi - the cross referencer service to use.
      type - the type which contain method.
      methodName - name of the method.
      signature - method signature.
      Returns:
      a list of related methods.
      Since:
      0.72

    • getAllRelatedMethods

      public static List<Method> getAllRelatedMethods(CrossReferenceSourceInfo xrsi, Method method)
      Query that returns a list of methods that redefine, implement or are overriden or implemented each other starting from method method . There are some cases where related methods might be outside of descendants or ascendants of type containing method. For instance, Collection.size() is related to Dictionary.size(), because Hashtable extends Dictionary and indirectly implements Collection .
      Parameters:
      xrsi - the cross referencer service to use.
      method - a method.
      Returns:
      a list of related methods including method.
      Since:
      0.72

    • getAllRelatedMethods

      public static List<Method> getAllRelatedMethods(NameInfo ni, CrossReferenceSourceInfo xrsi, ClassType type, String methodName, List<Type> signature)
